lung-visualization.name: Lung Healing Journey

0.text: Hour 1: Carbon monoxide is leaving your bloodstream. Oxygen levels are already rising.

1.text: Day 3: Your bronchial tubes are relaxing. Breathing becomes easier. Energy increases.

2.text: Week 2: Cilia — the tiny hairs in your lungs — are regrowing. They sweep out mucus and debris.

3.text: Month 1: Lung function has increased measurably. Coughing and shortness of breath decrease.

4.text: Month 9: Your lungs are dramatically cleaner. Infection risk has dropped. You can feel the difference.

5.text: Year 1: Your risk of heart disease is now HALF that of a smoker. Your body chose healing. You let it.

0.name: Head & Mind

1.name: Gut & Core
1.prompt: Opioid withdrawal hits the gut hard — cramping, nausea. Breathe into this area. This sensation is temporary and means your body is recalibrating.

3.name: Skin & Nerves
3.prompt: Goosebumps, sweating, sensitivity — your nervous system is resetting. Touch something textured. Ground into sensation.

4.name: Chest & Heart
4.prompt: Anxiety or physical tightness? Place both hands here. Slow your breathing. Your heart rate is normalizing with each sober day.

0.text: Day 1: Your dopamine receptors are nearly empty. Everything feels grey. This is the crash — not your new reality.

1.text: Week 1: Your brain begins upregulating receptors. Small pleasures start registering — food, warmth, a funny moment.

2.text: Week 2: Anhedonia is fading. Music sounds better. Sunsets have color again. Your reward system is rebuilding.

3.text: Month 1: Natural dopamine spikes from exercise, achievement, and connection feel real again.

4.text: Month 3: Your baseline has shifted. Normal life has texture and reward. The grey phase is behind you.

gaba-breathing.name: GABA Activation Breathing
gaba-breathing.description: This specific breathing ratio activates your GABA system — the same pathway benzos target.

0.text: Inhale through your nose for 4 counts. Fill your belly, not your chest.

1.text: Hold gently for 2 counts. No strain.

2.text: Exhale through pursed lips for 8 counts. The long exhale is the key — it stimulates your vagus nerve.

3.text: Pause for 2 counts. Let your body settle before the next breath.

benzo-bodyscan.name: Withdrawal Body Scan
benzo-bodyscan.description: Map where withdrawal is showing up today. Awareness reduces the fear.

0.name: Head

1.name: Eyes & Vision
1.prompt: Visual disturbances, light sensitivity? Your visual cortex is hyperexcitable without benzos dampening it. This normalizes.

2.name: Muscles
2.prompt: Tension, twitching, jaw clenching? Your muscles forgot how to relax naturally. Gentle stretching helps them remember.

3.name: Stomach
3.prompt: Nausea, loss of appetite? Your gut has GABA receptors too. They\

4.name: Nervous System
4.prompt: Tingling, burning skin, internal vibrations? Your nervous system is unmuting itself. Each day the volume turns down slightly.
